title: $SERVER_SECRET
text: $SERVER_SECRET Specifies a unique string to be used as a key in calculating the hash-values used to protect non-editable fields from being modified in a web browser. $SERVER_SECRET { = } Value Values Value —unique string, with a maximum length of 369 characters. The string should be long, complex, and hard to guess; for example, at least 64 characters and a combination of upper and lowercase characters, numbers, and symbols. Defaults Assignment file: wasv.asn Section: [WEB] Default value: Generated during installation Description To protect parts of the data that the DSP sends to the browser, such as NED fields and occurrence IDs, the server uses the $server_secret setting. The server protects NED fields with a hash-value that it generates using the $server_secret setting. On an AJAX request, this hash-value is sent back with the field data to the server, the server recalculates the hash-value, and compares it with the original. If the hash-values are the same, the field data is unchanged and accepted by the server. If the hash-values are not the same, a security error is generated, and any further server-side processing should stop. During installation, Uniface generates a unique key and assigns it to this setting, but you can provide your own key. CAUTION: The key should be kept secret, so you need to control access to the assignment file of the Web Application Server (by default, wasv.asn ) so that only the Uniface Server ( userver ) process can read it. For example: [WEB] $SERVER_SECRET = JIOhcvjsqj8vsadv;394hwe028*&^ijhe8762l;v93kns2-0gvr;b-05nm2k3;lkcv;flb0-76-u76-0g45nj348h&^Z Copy Related Links Web Application Configuration and Deployment When you deploy and configure the web application you need to ensure that the application is securely deployed. To do so, the passwords and security mechanisms provided with Uniface must be changed and secured. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
